plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure slightly.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad index exposure, enhancing stability.
Buy GOOGL as a top-quality tech name, adding diversification within tech.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to absorb potential market volatility.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a ~1.5% monthly return, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Risk is minimized by favoring broad indices and top-quality names.

plan: Trim SPY to comply with concentration limits and reduce overall broad index weight.
Sell SOXL due to its flagged status and high leverage, reducing risk.
Trim SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure, keeping it small.
Reduce QQQI due to its downtrend, reallocating towards stronger setups.
Increase SMH exposure, leaning into its uptrend in semiconductors.
Buy GOOGL, a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner read, for growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth, prioritizing risk management. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad indices with quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the single-name concentration mandate, freeing up capital.
Sell SOXL to exit the flagged position, despite the aggressive stance.
Trim QQQI due to its downtrend, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Increase SMH and CRWD exposure, leaning into their uptrends in tech and AI.
Buy GOOGL and RDDT, both top-scoring scanner setups, to maximize growth potential.
This plan uses concentration and strong setups to drive aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth, accepting significantly higher risk. It leans into concentration and strong scanner setups, including leveraged ETFs. Expect larger drawdowns and be aware of leveraged ETF decay, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reallocate to income-generating assets.
Significantly increase QQQI exposure to maximize monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to shift focus from growth to stable income generation.
Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing stability.
This plan prioritizes steady distributions and capital preservation over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions, focusing on parking cash for yield. It heavily favors income ETFs and broad indices, cutting high-beta and leveraged positions. While aiming for steady income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
